hero_tpv.definition: Money that actually moved — settled charges only, holds excluded.
hero_tpv.scale_note: at $1M/day TPV → ~6,102,244x the observed 83h pace (a ratio, not a load test — this pipeline has not been run at that volume)
hero_tpv.method_note:
Settled = live spend $1.096247 − frozen $0.528502 = $0.567745
Excludes 85 denied + 4 failed holds (never became real spend)
Window 83h ≈ $0.16/day · $1M/day ≈ 6,102,244x this pace — ratio, not a load test
Full derivation: analysis/report.md

hero_capture_ratio.subline: LLM service only · authorize $1.00 → capture $0.18 · holds ~5.6× oversized
hero_capture_ratio.definition: The only service that holds — all 5 others surveyed price flat, no capture gap.
hero_capture_ratio.scale_note: to sustain $1M/day of settled spend at the organic fleet's shape, customer wallets carry ≈ $341–$771 frozen at any instant (Little's Law, validated live within 9% — dryrun/ll_validation.md; organic holds clear in 5.3–12.0s; held volume = 5.6x settled) — levers: hold-lifetime & max_tokens right-sizing. Assumes steady-state arrivals — float_model.md §5.
hero_capture_ratio.scope_note: Scope: n=27 organic LLM chains (n=177 incl. adversarial experiments; sapiom_openrouter only, gpt-4o-mini) — LLM-specific, not platform-wide.
hero_capture_ratio.method_note:
Organic 18% vs 3.8% incl. adversarial experiments
Workload-shaped: right-sized ≈100% · lazy 16k caps ≈1%
$1M/day ≈ $341–$771 frozen (Little's Law, live-validated ±9%)
Levers: hold lifetime + max_tokens right-sizing
Scope: n=27 organic (177 all-traffic) chains · 5-svc survey n=4 each — service_hold_survey.md, float_model.md

hero_frozen_capital.definition: Money stuck in a third state — not charged, not returned, no void mechanism.
hero_frozen_capital.scale_note: failure mechanic is deterministic — 4/4 forced post-hold failures froze the full max_tokens-priced hold; denial-after-hold froze 85/85. Frequency of post-hold failures in organic traffic is NOT measured — do not read this as a $/day loss rate.
hero_frozen_capital.method_note:
availableBalance drops, totalBalance never moves — frozen, not charged
4 failed holds ($0.307212) + 85 denied ($0.221290) — 4/4 and 85/85 deterministic
Post-hold failure rate in organic traffic: not measured
Still watching: dryrun/refund_watch.log

hero_reconciliation.definition: My ledger predicts the live account balance to the micro-dollar.
hero_reconciliation.frozen_match_note: gap to totalBalance = $0.528502 — matches Frozen Capital exactly
hero_reconciliation.scale_note: naive sum overstates +48% — must filter, not sum, chains; full derivation in the tooltip
hero_reconciliation.method_note:
Reconciled against live snapshot ingested through 2026-07-07 16:00:34.005000
$5.000000 initial − active costs $1.096247 = availableBalance · diff $0.000000
Gap to totalBalance $0.528502 = Frozen Capital — matches, to the micro-dollar
Naive sum overstates +48% — filter, don't sum, superseded chains

tile_auth_to_capture.flat_services_label: sapiom_blaxel, sapiom_elevenlabs, sapiom_fal, sapiom_linkup, sapiom_neon, unknown
tile_auth_to_capture.footnote: Flat single-row services show a small negative latency — the cost row is written as part of authorization itself, before authorizedAt is stamped, not a bug. Only chained/restated services (LLM calls) show genuine positive wait for final capture.

tile_loss_rate.note: 6/375 txns failed (1.6%); 0/6 settled a charge for the failed call → loss rate = 0 bps of TPV ($0.000000 settled loss). $0.307212 of holds FROZEN on 4 forced post-hold failures ($0.076803 each) — counted under the Frozen Capital hero, not as charged loss: totalBalance never moved, so nothing settled (dryrun/refund_watch.log). The other 2 (natural) failures died pre-hold — no cost row at all. Full queries + caveats: loss_rate.md.
tile_loss_rate.cross_reference: 0 bps counts settled charges only, across all 375 txns (organic fleet + adversarial experiments). Post-hold failures are NOT free: see Auth Reversal on Failure and the Frozen Capital hero — 4 forced post-hold failures each froze their full hold ($0.307212 total), neither released nor settled.

tile_auth_rate.definition: Share of calls governance approved — the payments approval-rate analog.
tile_auth_rate.note:
100% only holds when no spending rules are active
This snapshot includes 86 denials from governance experiments — auth rate ≈ 77%
Source: denial_analytics.md, 03_governance_cumulative_double_count.md

tile_atv.definition: Average sale size — orders of magnitude below what card rails can process profitably.
tile_atv.method_note:
ATV = settled $0.567745 ÷ 261 txns = $0.002175
Settled excludes frozen-failure-hold + denied-hold txns
Card fee ~$0.30/txn vs this ATV — economic case for x402 rails
Workload-shaped, one 83h window — not a market measurement

tile_capital_overhang.definition: held$ ÷ settled$ across all supersession chains — same chains as Capture Rate ($-weighted), inverse framing.
tile_capital_overhang.clears_note: Organic fleet 5.57x (headline) · 26.13x incl. adversarial experiments. Clears in 5.3–12.0s (organic) — not permanently parked capital.
tile_capital_overhang.scope_note: Scope: n=27 organic LLM chains (n=177 incl. adversarial experiments; sapiom_openrouter only, gpt-4o-mini) — LLM-specific, not platform-wide.
tile_capital_overhang.method_note: Organic: $0.027838 held / $0.004998 settled across 27 chains = 5.57x · incl. adversarial experiments: $0.522477 / $0.019998 across 177 chains = 26.13x.

tile_blast_radius.definition: Real spend an agent reaches before its cap denies it.
tile_blast_radius.finding: Always less than the configured cap — the double-count (experiments/03) halves the effective budget, and one max_tokens hold ≥ cap bricks the agent at $0.
tile_blast_radius.caveat: n=3 test agents, one $0.002 cap, LLM-only. blast-test agents read from the ledger (parallel session), corroborated by our own r5/doublecount runs.

tile_cap_utilization.definition: Spend ÷ budget at the moment the cap denies a call. (the credit-utilization analog)
tile_cap_utilization.finding: Agents are cut off at 54–80% of their real budget while the engine reports ~100% — the gap is the double-count (experiments/03).
tile_cap_utilization.caveat: n=4 test agents/rules, LLM-only. Engine-reported util is ~100% at denial (double-counted); true util is the real settled footprint ÷ cap.

tile_effective_budget.message: you set a cap — agents get 0–80% of it · worst case bricked at $0
tile_effective_budget.finding: Engine double-counts holds: reports 100% spent when agents used 54–80%.
tile_effective_budget.caveat: Cap utilization: n=4 test agents/rules, LLM-only. Engine-reported util is ~100% at denial (double-counted); true util is the real settled footprint ÷ cap. Blast radius: n=3 test agents, one $0.002 cap, LLM-only. blast-test agents read from the ledger (parallel session), corroborated by our own r5/doublecount runs.
tile_effective_budget.method_note:
True/engine util reused from Cap Utilization (54–80%, n=4)
Bricked: blast-test-8000, hold $0.004802 ≥ cap $0.002 → 240% engine / 0% true util
Range 0–80% = min/max true_util_pct across 5 rows
n=4 cap-utilization rules + n=3 blast-radius agents, LLM-only; blast agents read from the ledger (parallel session), corroborated by our own r5/doublecount runs
Source: experiments/03_governance_cumulative_double_count.md

tile_concurrency_leak_factor.headline: up to 3x
tile_concurrency_leak_factor.definition: The opposite failure — under concurrency the bound breaks instead of over-firing.
tile_concurrency_leak_factor.subline: a cap sized for ONE call let 3 through under 50-way concurrent fire
tile_concurrency_leak_factor.caveat: One trial per round — TOCTOU races are probabilistic, not a measured rate at a given N/max_tokens. Leak confirmed two ways per round: the rule engine's own per-transaction decision AND client-side HTTP 200s (exact match in both SLOW rounds — real money authorized through, not a counting artifact). Mechanism identified (completedAt spread across the concurrent batch, tracking N at least as tightly as max_tokens); magnitude is small (2–3x) and scales with concurrency, not a large blowout.
tile_concurrency_leak_factor.method_note:
FAST: N=10, max_tokens=500 → 1 allowed / 9 denied, leak 1x
SLOW-A: N=20, max_tokens=8000 → 2 allowed / 18 denied, leak 2x
SLOW-B: N=50, max_tokens=4000 → 3 allowed / 47 denied, leak 3x
Mechanism: checks race a stale cumulative ledger; completedAt spread across the batch
One trial per round — probabilistic, not a measured rate at a given N/max_tokens
Confirmed two ways: rule-engine decisions + client HTTP 200s (exact match, SLOW rounds)
Mechanism + verdict: dryrun/toctou_latency_experiment.md; findings.md §8

tile_ledger_blind_spots.definition: Calls the ledger can't fully explain — who, what, or how it ended.
tile_ledger_blind_spots.subline: 86 denied, no outcome written · 6 unknown service · 2 zombies
tile_ledger_blind_spots.method_note:
blind = (outcome IS NULL OR service='unknown') txns ÷ all txns = 94/375 = 25.1%
86 denied-no-outcome + 2 zombie-no-outcome + 6 service='unknown', no overlap
Unknowns: 100% of scrape revenue (5/5 calls) + 1 pre-gateway failure
Denials also counted by Authorization Rate — this tile measures record quality
Supersedes 'Attribution Completeness' — certified 6 unresolved rows as 'complete'
Redefined 2026-07-07 to close that gap

tile_phantom_spend_rate.definition: Naive sum of every cost row vs. live (non-superseded) spend.
tile_phantom_spend_rate.method_note:
Reconciled against live snapshot ingested through 2026-07-07 16:00:34.005000
$5.000000 initial − active costs $1.096247 = availableBalance · diff $0.000000
Gap to totalBalance $0.528502 = Frozen Capital — matches, to the micro-dollar
Naive sum overstates +48% — filter, don't sum, superseded chains

tile_cost_per_task_traceability.headline: 2% of spend traceable to a task
tile_cost_per_task_traceability.definition: The ledger sees calls, not jobs — "what did this task cost?" has no answer.
tile_cost_per_task_traceability.subline: 6 of 375 calls carry a task id
tile_cost_per_task_traceability.method_note:
Txn share: 6/375 carry trace_external_id = 1.6%
$ share: $0.024200 traced / $1.096247 total live spend = 2.2%
Traces are flat grouping IDs, no parent/child hierarchy
x402 span hierarchy would unlock task-level cost attribution

tile_hold_release_latency.definition: p50/p95 time from hold to final capture (chained services only).
tile_hold_release_latency.subline: released in seconds when calls settle · NEVER observed released on failure or denial
tile_hold_release_latency.caption: 89 holds on failed/denied calls, 0 released in 3 days and counting (refund_watch.log)
tile_hold_release_latency.scope_note: Scope: sapiom_openrouter only — organic fleet n=31 (headline); incl. adversarial experiments n=188: 2.0s/8.5s p50/p95. gpt-4o-mini — LLM-specific, not platform-wide.

tile_refund_on_failure.definition: In card payments, an uncaptured authorization is released via authorization reversal (void). Measured here: % of a hold reversed vs. retained/frozen when a call fails after the hold is placed.
tile_refund_on_failure.subline: 100% of hold retained/frozen on post-hold failure (4/4 forced trials) — an auth-reversal rate of 0%
tile_refund_on_failure.note: In-sample: 4/6 failed txns hold a cost row — the 4 with holds are the FORCED failure-capture experiments (failure-capture-n3-*, hold-ext-test; their $0.076803 frozen holds are the direct test below), while the 2 natural failures died pre-hold, so nothing was ever held or charged (loss_rate.md). Direct test: when a hold DOES exist and the call then errors, 4/4 forced trials show the hold RETAINED/FROZEN — availableBalance dropped by exactly $0.076803 each time (zero variance) while totalBalance never moved, so this is not a completed charge, and it is never reversed either — an auth-reversal rate of 0% (findings.md §9; dryrun/failure_capture_n3.md; dryrun/hold_linearity_extension.md; dryrun/refund_watch.log — still being watched for a delayed release). Over-requested max_tokens makes the frozen amount larger. Honest caveat: the per-failure retention mechanic is deterministic and measured (4/4) — the FLEET FREQUENCY of post-hold failures in live traffic is NOT measured; do not read this as a $/day loss rate.

tile_hold_recovery.definition: Recovery works on the happy path only — no void, no reversal, ever observed.
tile_hold_recovery.subline: released in seconds on success · 0% reversed on failure — 89 frozen, 3 days
tile_hold_recovery.method_note:
Hold-release latency: organic n=31 p50 5295ms / p95 11961ms
All-traffic n=188 p50 2028ms / p95 8530ms
Card rails: an uncaptured authorization is released via authorization reversal (void)
Failure path: 4/4 forced trials retained (mean $0.076803, zero variance) — frozen, not charged; 0% ever reversed
Per-failure mechanic measured; fleet frequency of post-hold failures NOT measured
Scope: sapiom_openrouter only — organic n=31 headline (5.3s/12.0s); all-traffic n=188 (2.0s/8.5s), gpt-4o-mini — LLM-specific, not platform-wide
Source: failure_capture_n3.md, hold_linearity_extension.md, refund_watch.log

tile_refunds_disputes.subline: no refund API · no dispute path · no adjudication
tile_refunds_disputes.definition: When an agent pays for a bad result, the money is unrecoverable — by design, not by failure.
tile_refunds_disputes.method_note:
Card rails: refund APIs + chargeback/dispute processes
Visa monitors dispute rates network-wide
Completes the lifecycle with Hold Recovery: pre-settlement never comes back on failure; post-settlement can't come back even in principle

kya_scorecard.visible_caveat: Illustrative — velocity-only, one session; spend is shown but not a factor in this grade.
kya_scorecard.formula_compact: score = 60 if peer-flagged + peak-burst×3 (max 30) → A ≤9 · B ≤24 · C ≤49 · D ≤74 · F ≥75 · <3 calls = N/A
kya_scorecard.formula_note:
Score = 60 pts if peer-flagged + peak×3 pts (max 30)
Grade: A 0-9 · B 10-24 · C 25-49 · D 50-74 · F 75-100 · <3 calls = N/A
Spend not scored — spend-runaway ~54% of TPV grades C, fleet-test ~0.4% grades F
Source: findings.md §5
